content: Alignmas.
also made worse that althought Alignment sounds better than Align in almost all contexts, one specific context of "The time of Align is nigh" sounds a lot cooler to me without the -ment
nigh is just a cool word
Could change it to "The Alignment is nigh." maybe?
I'd also love to squeeze in a "The stars are right." somewhere, but only if it made sense.
My favorite part was when Miryam came in and shouted "Merry Alignmas, infidels!" and proceeded to Align everyone.
Also the game forcing me to use Ramona, but not letting me use GRamona is mean.
yeah the forced ramona/ramonaless stages are quite annoying
at least forced ramona stops after chapter...5? 6? i forget
i think there were ramonaless stages in 7 maybe...
but that's much less of an issue
"align everyone" sounds like she put people on the wall and rotated them
https://tenor.com/view/rotation-ratated-get-rotated-idiot-idiot-cat-gif-1040051056493706743
Wait.
I just went to check the Important Terms sheet again, Lens.
```Deep One-Teen    Male    凯刻斯    Caecus```
ah. read through that, yeah I guess it makes sense now why my vibes on the word were so rancid, I live in an ~~islamophobic hellhole~~
It **was** changed to Deep One.
oh huh
wait what am I missing
I don't see it
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1QW8qr1y7DLFnTZVOHUTmw_IBL9bq8JEaIPKQGMOjzKE/edit?gid=1786279439#gid=1786279439
https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1cKOGnLESM3WajWbTVEWdacvpuBfENQw1iQ9ep-ih9Mc/edit?gid=921939550#gid=921939550
I'm looking at that one.
oh wait you're referring to the naming sheet
that's for the source reference
like, pickman is a reference to Richard Upton Pickman
but we obviously don't call him Richard Upton Pickman ingame
Oh I see.
that's for us to know the reference of so we can reference the right influences
Gotcha. Got my wires crossed~
like the japanese version for instance has some conflicts cuz of it

also very funny that I had *literally actually* no idea what Perishite meant, and since it was only ever used specifically by Miryam when speaking to or about Goliath, I assumed it was either (a) his true name, or (b) *ironically,* a made up in-universe racial slur *(using "perish" to mean she believes they/their civilization should all die)*
Celeste is obviously based off the Mary Celeste, right, which is セレスト is japanese, so it'd be most correct to translate her name as that
but chatgpt made her name シレステ
and funnily enough, the actual JP voicelines that the voice actors they hired say シレスト
because i'm assuming all the lines they were fed were just chatgpt'd
Big Reverse 1999 moment.

so now the poor jp version is stuck with an obvious reference that gets missed, a voiceline that's obviously wrong, and an ingame name that doesn't match what the characters are actually saying out loud
big oof
Except in R1999 they were just going off the original script that was broken English written by a Chinese studio that thought maybe a bit too highly of how fluent they were.
it feels bad for the JP players since they're literally talking in japanese in the game so it's fully understandable to them
whereas to english players the jp dialogue is just cool sounding but it's not like you'd know if it was wrong
oof, rip
to be fair, that would be quite on-brand for Miryam honestly
Yeah, as usual every time a company employs AI as a cost-saving measure, it ends up creating a snowball of problems that far outweigh what it would've cost to do it properly the first time around.

yeahhhh, in the TCG community a huge name in game accessories Ultimate Guard just learned that the HAAAARD way
they licensed a magic artist's official art and then decided to save about $500 extending the art with genAI to fit their deck box instead of just paying the original artist to extend the art
that was about 2 weeks ago, their company is already starting to fall apart at the seams as the magic community RIPS THEM A NEW ONE

HOWEVER;
ASSUMING I understand correctly the timeline of events for Morimens over the last year, personally I agree with the usage of AI by BIAV here. *(PLEASE correct me if I'm wrong, but)* It sounded like they were in pretty dire straits after the 1st anniversary, and putting an "acceptable for now" job on getting their game to an international audience seems to have brought them the resources needed to continue development and even, admirably, allocate funds to hiring another translator to do a much better job than what they could afford the first time around.
yeah I don't fault them at all for the AI stuff
because well
the studio is what, like literally half a dozen people or something

I thought it was a simultaneous release? I might very well be wrong here, as I only became aware of the game when Pseychie released his video, and I only decided to give it a shot when I heard news of the human translation being introduced.
(I have no idea but however many employees they have it's clearly not many)
nope, international release was an EoS-risk desperation move to try and get a bigger audience
same here, I only made it 13 minutes into Pseychie's video before going "SAY NO MORE" and turning it off to download the game
they legit had me at "It's like Reverse: 1999 but doesn't suck"
Aaah I see. That makes more sense then.
but yeah a pure chinese company with probably a single-digit number of employees, none of whom speak english at a fluent level
without AI this game never would've made it to us
so I'm more or less right about what I understand about the subject?
so I'm totally onboard with it
well I also wasn't around for it so I'm going off what I've heard in the discord
same lmao.
I think flares had a video about the history of it

pretty much the only info I have on the matter is vague bits of mentions of it here and there, and the 20ish minute Flares video that I did not watch because the pinned comment was a response from Light saying "hey you're wrong about what you said, please don't spread misinformation, here's what happened"
oh lmfao
oops that's my fault for not reading youtube comments
oh, interesting
okay so ignore everything i ever said then, i am as uneducated as everyone else
but yeah more or less the jist I got from that comment is what you said here
and it seems like it worked pretty well! I'm so stoked to be a part of this community and help the devs out in whatever way I can
real fun hobby these last few days
yeah
it's so funny to me when they say "please don't make sweeping changes bc it's way too much work" like yeah okay that's the point? try to stop me lmao
I'm gonna do it whether you like it or not, it's up to you if you wanna use it in the end, I'm having a blast

pretty much every point Ace has made can be read as a point in my favor
> the CN devs want to rewrite all the cards to make more sense first, but don't have the time to right now
okay cool, so if I write out a comprehensive guide to how it should be written in english that should help at least a little to inform the devs on what steps to take in their rewrite
> once it's rewritten in CN it would need to be translated back into english again
cool! call me up when that happens if I'm useful. like you said, Lens, this is an iterative process
> if we put 10% of the fix live and the other 90% isn't done then that would cause more confusion
of course, that's why I'm trying to get it all done at once and present it completed no matter how long that takes me
> we need to be careful with Awakener skills because, well, people pay for those
Case in point, there are a few Awakeners that I guarantee would sell a lot better if you could read them and know what they do lol
(looking at you, Horla)
(actually I'm not looking at Horla, last time I tried I got a migraine)
Playing through Chapter 6, and my current pet peeve is calling it "The Align"...
YES
UGH
fix pls
The new hires will have to do that~
oof
I'll focus on the stuff they won't go through, so the Miryam story next. I'm just trying to push the story a bit so I don't spoil myself every time I work on a new side story.
Kathigu-ra's story for example says I should wait until Chapter 8.
ahhhh
yeah I haven't done any side stories yet lol
Daffodil's was nice and has a veeery interesting bit of foreshadowing. I didn't care for 24's or Lily's, other than the endings for both are very heartwarming. Sorel's was really good too.
Ogier's is short and bittersweet, unfortunately brought down by the machine translation. I wouldn't mind taking a look at it after Miryam's if no one else has picked it up.
I didn't much care for GRamona's, but maybe I just had very high expectations.
Considering (as far as I know) you have to do Ogier's before you can do anyone else's I feel like that should get prioritized by someone lol
i'm almost done with ch5 and the align has been a source of conflict for me
it's a very clearly named term that has to be capitalized but also there's not really a good term I can think of that'd be equivalent
Yeah, I agree. It's also I think the shortest of all the side stories, so not too bad.
The Alignment.
so far i've been using some variation of "Alignment" and "time of Alignment" and "Stellar Alignment" and "Alignment of the stars" depending on context
but it's slightly frustrating to me because i'd like a single good term that sounds good in all contexts but that's never going to exist
Yeah, I think all of those are better than "The Align"
Clearly it should be Alignment's Eve.
Better yet.

completely and entirely irrelevant but I just realized I should probably stop pronouncing Morimens as if it were a Japanese word
I just consume so much jp-language media that I've gotten used to pronouncing anything anime that way (especially any Rs)
but it only occurred to me just now that it's very likely that the name Morimens is a play on Memento Mori *("-men-" as in to think, Mori from root "mer-" as in to die or be harmed).* I would love some confirmation of this lol because if that's the case that's dope as hell
it is
but there are like
2 other gacha games that are either literally that (MementoMori the literal AFK game) and
damn I don't remember the other one but there's another turn based game that uses the naming convention
lol
idk I think this one nails it way harder than any other
*tbh it kinda makes me think of Mori fashion*

Memento Mori literally meaning "to think of death" reflects the theme of dissolution and our role as some of the few people *capable* of "thinking" of "the dead" per se
double entendre with Awakeners being *technically* undead yet thinking
it's either that or mori = die, mens = mind
either way it's a little bit unfortunate because the chinese name is 忘却前夜 which means "forgetting the previous night"
which i believe Ace translated as "Ere Oblivion's Dawn"
Yeah, I saw there was a "Forget Last Night Wiki" and I thought that was fire
but it does pop up in a few places throughout the story and every time it's like
oh hey that's the name of the game!
https://tenor.com/view/thats-it-yes-thats-it-that-right-there-omg-that-thats-what-i-mean-gif-17579879
....except it doesn't in English
because there's no way to work "Morimens" into an english sentence and have it make sense
lmao yeah
these games tend to be like that lol, classic example is "wtf is a genshin" when yuanshen is legible in chinese

like it's not exactly a major point or anything but beating the arc 1 story and sitting through all the cool cinematics and super cool ending and then going back to the story mode page and seeing a tab that says ~Morimens~ is a helluva nice feeling

And together... We Are Morimens
*Avengers theme start playing*
should have put it has spoiler
lol
also as a concept it just gets referenced a lot in ch9 so it's kinda sad that that's not really conveyable in english
the ending theme vocals are like, built around that phrase but it's in english so that meaning is just entirely lost
but ah well
a lot of wordplay in foreign languages is just untranslateable so what can you do
Can't be worse than when Genshin Impact introduced the concept of people that can ascend to godhood, called it "Genshin" in JP... but "Allogenes" in EN.
Like it was right there. The end of the prologue. Dropping in the meaning of the title.
Nope, let's use a different term for it altogether.
I think I'm going to start poking at Miryam's story tonight. Maybe work on a segment per day then take a day off then back again so I don't burn myself out.
I'll wait to read it until your work goes live!
iirc there were a lot of lines in there that didn't really make any sense in context so ping me if you get confused by any of the meanings
also her story has a lot of terminology so we should try to keep it aligned
fwiw i've been using "fishmen" rather than murlocs since we already got the go-ahead from Ace to change that term and "merfolk/mermen" kind of has an established impression of human top
whereas fishmen isn't an established phrase but lizardmen definitely is and that's a race of lizard top human bottom so it fits the structure well
also the chinese term for them is uh, quite literally just "fish people" so it's the closest equivalent I can think of
but yeah going through the story makes me realize how much of the chatgpt machine translation is just....completely wrong
and it makes me kinda pessimistic about just doing pure editing passes since it's going to stay wrong if not TL checked

usually for non-traditional merfolk (i.e. not strictly human waist-up fish waist-down) I see the term "Merrow" used, but that definitely does NOT fit the mood here
I would describe them as "abominations" before I describe them as Merrow <:PAIMON_DEAD:708170044848472105>
fortunately I can TL check the main arc 1 story as I go but the workload is too much for me to do event/side stories as well so I'm hoping the quality doesn't suffer too much from lack of a TL check
so yeah fishmen should definitely be used to describe the *individuals, in appearance* though I still am quite partial to referring to the whole as Seafarer or (the) Seafarer

obligatory Keeper line because I still find it absolutely hilarious
> Those hideous faces; just looking at them feels like an insult to evolution.
yup
Seafarer is explicitly the name of the religious cult, so all members of it are referred to as Seafarers
I guess technically it'd be like, one who traverses the seas/walks upon the seas but Seafarers seems to be the established term and it works fine imo so that's what I've been going with
I assume you've been adding the plural suffix then? I kinda liked that it was never present. I never once noticed the word "Seafarer**s**" used and it felt fitting to me
Didn't Ace change some of the terminology in the sheet? I think Caecus was called Murloc Youth but is now called Deep One Youth.
yep
I'm fond of Deep Ones myself because that's just the actual origin of that archetype.
But I'm fine with fishmen too, or alternating.
Maybe Deep Ones when it's Miryam or the Lemurian Cult/Seafarers referring to them.
nope, I checked the sheet and it's still murlocs atm
And fishmen when it's the Keeper or the "Philistines"
I did weigh the idea of using Deep Ones but I ended up consciously deciding against it

primarily because while deep ones are very extremely obviously the source reference for the seafarers, it's also so obvious that if the devs had wanted them to be directly called deep ones, they would have called them deep ones
Also, no clue what to call the Philistines.
I agree with that
Because they're clearly not literally Philistines.
there's a beauty in keeping the references as references without just directly pushing it in your face
uhh, what do you mean
"And thus we summon the Deep Ones, from the Cthulhu Mythos of H. P. Lovecraft."
https://tenor.com/view/anime-anime-girl-head-nod-head-nodding-head-nod-yes-gif-24603507
it should be literally Philistines afaik
also I have very little idea what Philistine means and I'm gonna look it up but it sounds like a racial slur lmao
they're a historical civilization
like "barbarian"
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philistines

content: same as the Arameans in ch7
That's good to know.
a lot of these terms are like, the actual words
which is why i didn't use deep ones
Also, I love that they were called what was it... "Pharigites" or something along those lines interchangeably. Which is I think the Japanese term for Philistines?
cuz they clearly have no issues with using actual terms when they want to directly mention it so if they had wanted deep ones I do feel like it'd have been deep ones directly
there's like 3 different terms for them because machine translation
but yeah Perishites
ペリシテ人
Yeah, I figured pretty quickly that it just meant Philistines, but definitely fixing that.

content: Perishites yeah, that's the one.
Yeah, that's the wiki page it lead me to when I searched it.
(I also think Perishites sounds awesome. Shame that it's just the Japanese term.)
